corpus.title: German chancellor announces plan to purchase US Tomahawk missiles
corpus.signal_text:
German chancellor announces plan to purchase US Tomahawk missiles

Berlin agreed with Washington during NATO's Ankara summit to buy US Tomahawks and station them in Germany, helping it close strategic defense gap, Merz tells parliament

corpus.title: Tomahawk-Deal mit Trump perfekt - Deutschland kauft jetzt Anti-Putin-Raketen

Tomahawk-Deal mit Trump perfekt - Deutschland kauft jetzt Anti-Putin-Raketen

Deutschland kauft Tomahawk-Marschflugkörper und schließt eine strategische Lücke.

corpus.title: Merz: Germany agreed to buy Tomahawk missiles at NATO summit
corpus.signal_text:
Merz: Germany agreed to buy Tomahawk missiles at NATO summit
Published July 9, 2026last updated July 9, 2026Chancellor Friedrich Merz has informed the Bundestag parliament of a deal to buy US Tomahawk cruise missiles and station them in Germany. He said this closed a "important strategic gap in our defense."
Germany has agreed to purchase US Tomahawk cruise missiles and station them in Germany, Chancellor Friedrich Merz told the Bundestag parliament on Thursday morning.
Merz has just returned from the NATO summit in Ankara, Turkey, also attended by US President Donald Trump and other senior US government officials.
What did Merz tell parliament after the NATO summit?
Merz was speaking in a broader address to the Bundestag touting his government's record.
As well as a string of domestic and economic priorities — like planned income tax and pension reforms working their way through parliament — he listed a final goal as moving toward "a future where our country is not susceptible to blackmail, but rather can confidently meet every threat posed to our free way of life using its own strength."
Merz said the "results" of this week's NATO summit in Turkey "exceeded all of my expectations," saying it had showed that the alliance was "united, strong and self-confident."
"On the sidelines of the NATO summit in Ankara we also agreed with the American government that Tomahawk missiles would be purchased by us and stationed in Germany," Merz said. "With this we are closing an important strategic gap in our defense. And at the same time we will work on devloping our own European systems and deploying them in Europe."
Merz also touted the deal reached this week to sell TKMS submarines to Canada as the largest military contract in modern German history.
He said that agreements such as these "make it clear to me that NATO is, and remains, a trans-Atlantic alliance."
"But we as Europeans are also strong, and we have understood that we cannot simply outsource our security," he told the chamber in Berlin.
What else did Merz discuss in the speech?
Merz's reports from his recent sojourn in Ankara were one part of a broader address dedicated for the most part to domestic politics.
The Christian Democrat chancellor was trying to drum up support for his coalition's package of summer reforms in various areas, like pensions, welfare, health care and income tax.
"These agreements aim above all for more flexibility for our businesses, for the preservation of our welfare state, for ta

corpus.title: US approves sale of Tomahawk missiles to Germany, says Chancellor Merz
corpus.signal_text:
US approves sale of Tomahawk missiles to Germany, says Chancellor Merz

In May Merz suggested that a planned deployment of Tomahawk missiles to Germany -- announced by former US president Joe Biden -- was being called off.

corpus.title: Merz: gli Stati Uniti hanno approvato la vendita di Tomahawk alla Germania
corpus.signal_text:
Merz: gli Stati Uniti hanno approvato la vendita di Tomahawk alla Germania
Il cancelliere tedesco Merz conferma l'accordo con Washington per la stazionamento dei Tomahawk in Germania, evidenziando il ruolo attivo di Berlino nella sicurezza europea e la coesione Nato
1' di lettura
1' di lettura
Gli Stati Uniti hanno approvato la vendita alla Germania dei Tomahawk. Lo ha affermato il cancelliere tedesco Friedrich Merz, che parlando al Bundestag ha riferito di un “accordo” con gli americani.
Chiedilo al Sole
«I Tomahawk potranno essere stazionati in Germania», ha aggiunto. «Così chiudiamo un importante buco nella nostra difesa», ha affermato Merz.
Merz: «Vertice Ankara ha superato aspettative»
Il risultato del vertice Nato di Ankara «ha superato tutte le mie aspettative. La Nato è unita, forte e fiduciosa. E vediamo infine che anche i membri europei, insieme al Canada, hanno riconosciuto la necessità di fare molto di più per la propria difesa».
Lo ha detto il cancelliere tedesco, sempre nel suo intervento al Bundestag. «La Germania ha assunto qui un ruolo importante, in stretto coordinamento con i nostri vicini in Europa. Raggiungeremo l’obiettivo del 5% ben prima del termine previsto», ha detto ancora Merz, sottolineando che «la Nato è e rimane un’alleanza transatlantica, ma anche noi europei siamo forti e abbiamo capito che non possiamo semplicemente delegare la nostra sicurezza ad altri. Della nostra sicurezza siamo noi stessi responsabili».

corpus.title: Мерц заявил о согласии США продать Германии ракеты Tomahawk
corpus.signal_text:
Мерц заявил о согласии США продать Германии ракеты Tomahawk

США одобрили продажу Германии крылатых ракет большой дальности Tomahawk. Об этом сообщил канцлер Фридрих Мерц во время выступления в парламенте, передает AFP.

corpus.title: Einigung mit den USA: Deutschland kauft "Tomahawk"-Marschflugkörper
corpus.signal_text:
Einigung mit den USA: Deutschland kauft "Tomahawk"-Marschflugkörper

Die Einigung mit den USA verkündet Kanzler Merz in seiner Regierungserklärung. Washington hat dem Verkauf von "Tomahawk"-Marschflugkörpern an Deutschland zugestimmt.

corpus.title: USA verkaufen Tomahawk-Raketen an Deutschland
corpus.signal_text:
USA verkaufen Tomahawk-Raketen an Deutschland

Einen Termin für die Stationierung gibt es bisher nicht, aber nun steht fest, dass Deutschland von den Vereinigten Staaten Tomahawk-Raketen kaufen kann. Das teilte der Kanzler in seiner Regierungserklärung am Donnerstag mit.

corpus.title: Einigung auf Kauf von Tomahawk-Marschflugkörpern
corpus.signal_text:
Einigung auf Kauf von Tomahawk-Marschflugkörpern

Deutschland hat sich mit den USA auf den Kauf von Mittelstreckenwaffen des Typs Tomahawk geeinigt. Das sagte Kanzler Merz (CDU) bei einer Regierungserklärung im Bundestag.
